title: Plagiarism in the Digital Age
creator: fortune
description: A student submits a major essay that contains large sections generated by ChatGPT and copied from online sources without proper citation. When confronted, the student says they used only grammarly, or run their work through AI to correct errrors, and enhance academic voice.  How should the TA respond in a developmental rather than purely punitive way?

title: AI-Generated Content vs. Student Voice
creator: fortune
description: During peer review of a personal narrative assignment, the TA notices that one student’s draft is unusually polished and lacks personal voice. The student later admits they used ChatGPT for the entire first draft. The TA must decide how to address AI use while supporting the student’s development of authentic voice.

title: Chronic Absences and Late Work
creator: fortune
description: A first-year student has already missed the attendance threshold due to mental health issues and a part-time job or reasons known best to them.They submit strong work when they attend but are now at risk of failing due to ASU’s attendance policy. The student asks for flexibility.

title: Multilingual Writer Challenges
creator: fortune
description: An international student submits a strong idea-rich draft with significant grammar and syntax issues. The TA is unsure how much to focus on “correctness” versus rhetorical strengths, especially with revision opportunities ahead.

title: Student Resistance to Personal Writing
creator: fortune
description: In a literacy narrative assignment, a student refuses to share personal experiences, saying the prompt feels too invasive and “this isn’t therapy.” The TA must respond while maintaining engagement with the assignment goals.

title: Disruptive Technology Use in Class
creator: fortune
description: During peer review and discussion, several students are openly using phones and social media. One student defends it as helping them focus. The TA needs to establish and enforce effective classroom technology norms.

title: Grade Complaints and Rubric Interpretation
creator: fortune
description: A high-achieving student becomes emotional and argumentative about receiving a B+ on a project, claiming the rubric was applied unfairly. The conversation escalates quickly.

title: Group Project Dysfunction
creator: fortune
description: In a collaborative research project, one group member contributes almost nothing while the others do the majority of the work. The group asks the TA to intervene before the final submission
subject-tags: group-work; collaboration; fairness; project-management Arizona State

title: Time Management and Overwhelmed First-Years
creator: fortune
description: Many students in the class are juggling jobs, family responsibilities, and a heavy ASU course load. Several miss major deadlines. The TA wants to support them effectively without lowering standards or creating extra workload for themselves

title: Feedback Overload vs. Student Agency
creator: fortune
description: The TA gives extensive written feedback on student drafts, but many students make minimal revisions and appear overwhelmed. The TA wants to shift toward more process-oriented and metacognitive feedback that encourages student ownership.
